methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
SkySafe is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis leverages a peer set of 6 same-sector, same-stage private companies alongside 2 public comparables in the defense technology space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $50M and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $95M. The peer-median approach examines 6 Defense & Security Series B companies—Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and one additional peer—generating a median valuation of $1.2B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B.

The two methods produce a wide spread, with stage-progression at $95M and peer-median at $1.2B. Triangulating across both approaches yields a low of $81M, median of $648M, and high of $1.4B. The substantial gap between methods reflects tension between SkySafe's capital deployment relative to peer valuations in a capital-intensive, high-risk defense sector. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, customer concentration, and the degree to which peer valuations reflect market-driven pricing versus speculative froth in frontier defense tech. A narrowed range requires additional clarity on unit economics and sales momentum.
